factors.name: Hot US CPI / Real Yields
factors.summary: Headline CPI came in at 4.2% YoY vs 3.8% prior (per FXStreet), highest in 3 years. This surprise acceleration triggers Fed hawkish repricing; real yields (10y TIPS) are the single-biggest inverse driver of gold. FXStreet reports gold tumbled to ~$4,050 (multi-month low since Nov 2025) following the CPI print.

factors.name: US Dollar Index Strength
factors.summary: DXY firmed above 100.00 following inflation data (FXStreet), typical risk-off dollar strength that competes with gold as safe-haven. Stronger USD directly pressures XAUUSD via currency depreciation.

factors.name: Fed Hawkish Repricing
factors.summary: Hot inflation data reverses expectations for Fed easing; markets repricing for 'higher-for-longer' rates (per FXStreet JPY commentary). Higher forward real rates are structurally negative for non-yielding gold.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk / Safe-Haven Flows — Conflicting Signal
factors.summary: Major escalation: US launched second consecutive day of strikes on Iran; Iran formally closed Strait of Hormuz to all vessels; Hegseth threatened strikes 'tonight' (per multiple Al Jazeera/Bloomberg). This would normally trigger safe-haven bid in gold, but weak DXY + hot inflation is drowning it out.

factors.name: Oil Spike from Hormuz Closure
factors.summary: Brent/WTI surged on Hormuz closure threat; disruption of ~20% of global oil flows should fuel inflation persistence. Higher oil prices reinforce hawkish Fed expectations (negative for gold real yields), partially offsetting safe-haven bid.

factors.name: Near-Term Deal Chatter
factors.summary: Trump claimed Iran asked US to halt strikes and deal is near; this reduces near-term escalation premium. However, Hegseth's follow-up threats and Trump's retention of strike option keep geopolitical tail risk alive.

factors.name: Gold ETF / Institutional Flows
factors.summary: No explicit flow data in window, but sharp 2-3% drop in 24h typically precedes further outflows as technical levels break (e.g. $4,050 low triggers stops).

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No fresh CB buying announcements in news window. Structural bid from PBoC/RBI present but insufficient to counter macro headwinds.

screening.narrative: Gold is caught in a classic 'risk-off but still liquidating' squeeze: the US CPI surprise to 4.2% YoY has forcibly repriced Fed expectations higher, pushing real yields up and DXY above 100, both structural headwinds for gold. Simultaneously, US-Iran escalation (Hormuz closure, dual strike nights) should be a classic safe-haven trigger, but it is being overwhelmed by inflation persistence and hawkish Fed repricing. Gold has already fallen 2.98% in 24h and touched multi-month lows near $4,050, suggesting the move is in Phase 2 and momentum is to the downside. The geopolitical bid remains live if inflation fears ease OR if a deal is confirmed, but near-term technicals and macro flows favor further weakness.
catalysts_ahead: Trump/US officials' next escalation threat or de-escalation statement (hours; directly impacts safe-haven bid)
Iran response announcement (hours; could reignite Hormuz closure premium)
US Treasury/Fed speakers on inflation implications (next 24-48h; real yield update)
Next economic data: Asian data Thursday (light per Investinglive); US retail sales, jobless claims later this week
Any sign of Hormuz reopening or US-Iran deal confirmation (hours to days; major de-risk event)
screening.risk_warning: If Hormuz closure is confirmed as sustained (not bluff) OR if a major deal is announced to end Iran war within 24h, safe-haven flows and inflation persistence could flip gold back above $4,100. Conversely, if Fed speakers signal they will tolerate 4%+ inflation and pause rate hikes, real yields could compress and support gold despite DXY strength.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (already down 2.98% in 24h, touched multi-month lows). Avoid chasing shorts into $4,050; wait for retest of $4,080–$4,100 resistance before considering fresh short. If Hormuz escalation headlines spike again in next 4h, watch for a pullback-to-buy setup near $4,040–$4,050 support.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (US-Iran Escalation)
factors.summary: US-Iran military exchanges now on second consecutive day with Strait of Hormuz closure announced by Iran; Kuwait/Bahrain/Jordan targeted. Widening regional conflict typical of safe-haven demand for gold.

factors.name: Oil Price Spike
factors.summary: WTI crude near $91 per barrel on escalation fears; oil inventory drawdowns approaching operational minimums per InvestingLive analysis, signaling structural energy supply stress and inflation risk.

factors.name: Real Yields & Fed Rate Expectations
factors.summary: Hot US inflation report fueling expectations for higher-for-longer Fed rates; ECB set for first hike since 2023; higher real yields are typically headwind for gold valuations.

factors.name: US Dollar Index & Safe-Haven Flows
factors.summary: DXY trading around 100.00 but showing losses despite elevated geopolitical risk; USD typically competes with gold as safe-haven; JPY strengthening near 160.50 (6-week low) suggests yen carry still pressuring USD.

factors.name: Technical Breakdown
factors.summary: Gold broke below 200-day MA and March 2026 low ($4,099) on 2026-06-10; now trading at YTD lows per InvestingLive. Prior SELL6/10 bias remains active; price action is capitulative, not reversal-ready.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying (Structural Support)
factors.summary: Georgia CBR bought $100M more gold; global CBs purchased 970+ tons in Q1 2026 (~80% of 2025 annual pace); low price-sensitivity creates structural floor irrespective of near-term moves.

factors.name: Inflation Surprise & ECB Hike Ahead
factors.summary: Inflation appearing sticky (Trump noted 'loves inflation'); ECB rate decision today (2026-06-11 12:15 GMT per FXStreet). Hike would typically pressure gold but geopolitical premium may offset.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows & Institutional Positioning
factors.summary: No explicit data on GLD/IAU flows in news window; phase-2 capitulation often precedes large institutional repositioning, but no confirmation yet.

key_levels.support: $4,024 (early session low 2026-06-11); $3,950–$4,000 (YTD lows if breakdown extends)

screening.narrative: Gold experienced a sharp capitulative breakdown through the March 2026 low ($4,099) on 2026-06-10, closing well below the 200-day MA for the first time since October 2023. While geopolitical tensions (US-Iran escalation, Strait of Hormuz closure) and surging oil prices ($91 WTI) create classic safe-haven tailwinds, these are being offset by higher real-yield expectations: hotter-than-expected US inflation and the imminent ECB rate hike today are locking in a 'higher-for-longer' rates regime. The USD, despite geopolitical risk, shows resilience near DXY 100, and institutional carry unwinds (JPY strength near 160.50) suggest capital is rotating into rate-sensitive plays, not gold. The price action is in Phase 2: a rebound is underway from overnight lows ($4,024) to $4,080–$4,118, but the larger technical trend remains bearish and the move is not yet invalidating the prior SELL6/10 bias.
catalysts_ahead: ECB Rate Decision – 2026-06-11 12:15 GMT (likely hike; immediate EUR strength & wider risk-free rate could pressure gold)
US Producer Price Index (PPI) – expected 2026-06-11 (inflation data; beat = more bearish for gold long-term)
FOMC Meeting Minutes or Powell Speech – next 48-72h (Fed hawkishness = bearish gold)
Iran Ceasefire/Military Updates – ongoing (any de-escalation = negative for safe-haven bid; further escalation = positive but may already priced in)
Oil Inventory Data – API/EIA releases this week (if tightness confirmed, inflation expectations rise → bearish gold)
screening.risk_warning: If Strait of Hormuz physically closes for >48 hours or an imminent regional military escalation (Israel-Hezbollah, broader Iran alliance) emerges, the safe-haven bid could override rate headwinds and gold could spike $100+ despite higher real yields. Conversely, if ECB hike or any hawkish Fed signal triggers a sharp bond selloff (real yields rise to 2%+ in 10y TIPS), gold could accelerate toward $3,950. Do NOT assume the geopolitical premium is sustainable—markets historically struggle to price prolonged Hormuz disruptions accurately.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (rebound underway from lows); technical setup remains bearish (broken below March low). Wait for gold to retest $4,150–$4,180 (200-day MA) for a better short entry rather than chasing the bounce. If geopolitical fears worsen and gold breaks above $4,200 decisively on the day, flip bias to BUY, but do not add shorts into strength at this stage.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No fresh TIPS data in news window. Prior CPI print (described as 'mostly in line with expectations') suggests real yields remain range-bound; Fed pricing unchanged at 24bps tightening by year-end. Real yields remain the dominant headwind for gold, but no new shock.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy & Timing
factors.summary: FOMC meeting next week (mentioned as 'focus turns to FOMC'). Market still pricing hawkish hold; this creates pre-event uncertainty and caps gold upside. No dovish surprise evident; Fed likely to hold firm on inflation vigilance.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: USD strengthening on risk-off sentiment and Iran escalation uncertainty. Multiple pairs show USD strength (EUR/USD down near 2-month lows, AUD depressed, INR weakness). Stronger USD inversely pressures gold; DXY uptrend intact into FOMC per TD Securities.

factors.name: Oil Prices & Geopolitical Risk
factors.summary: US-Iran escalation spike: Strait of Hormuz closure threat, drone attacks, tanker strikes on three Indian sailors. Brent crude climbed toward $95/bbl. Oil is inflationary input; elevated oil typically supports gold as inflation hedge, though initial move saw oil decline as talks remained intact.

factors.name: Central Bank Actions & Forward Guidance
factors.summary: ECB hiking 25bps today to 2.25%, with downgraded growth projections (per multiple sources). Higher global rates compete with gold; BoC held at 2.25%, signaling limited near-term easing despite recession risks. Tightening bias globally pressures gold.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment & Safe-Haven Flows
factors.summary: Emerging-market selloff (6th down day in 7), equity futures bounced +0.8-1.3%, but underlying mood remains 'nervous.' Gold initially fell to lowest since Nov 2025, then recovered modestly. Safe-haven bid exists but masked by rising real rates and USD strength.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations
factors.summary: Latest CPI print 'in line with expectations'; no breakevens shock mentioned. However, AI boom cited as stoking chip inflation ('insane prices'), and Trump stated 'I love inflation'—signals inflation not yet conquered. Breakevens likely stable; no fresh expectations reset.

factors.name: Gold-Specific Flows & Positioning
factors.summary: Al Jazeera notes 'Gold is now the top reserve asset (27% of holdings), overtaking US Treasuries'—structural bullish narrative but already priced in. Silver rallied +1.05% to $64.05, outperforming gold slightly; GLD/IAU flows not mentioned; no institutional panic positioning evident.

factors.name: Price Action & Technicals
factors.summary: Gold hit lowest since Nov 2025 earlier today, then recovered to $4,111. 4h change -0.35%, 24h change +0.19% = choppy, low conviction. Move is incremental and exhausted; no follow-through momentum after initial recovery.

factors.name: Upcoming Catalysts Timing
factors.summary: ECB decision today (12:15 GMT, ~2h away), FOMC next week, US PPI data expected imminently. Imminent ECB hike likely priced in but guidance matters; FOMC next week caps upside convictiveness. Near-term event risk is high.

screening.narrative: Gold is caught in a tug-of-war between geopolitical inflation risk (US-Iran escalation, Strait of Hormuz closure threat, oil rally) and structural headwinds (rising global real rates via ECB hike today, persistent Fed hawkishness into next week's FOMC, strong USD). Price action shows exhaustion after hitting 7-month lows; modest recovery to $4,111 lacks conviction. The near-term catalyst overhang (ECB in hours, FOMC next week) and conflicting signals (safe-haven bid vs. real-yield selloff) keep bias stuck in neutral-to-choppy mode.
catalysts_ahead: ECB rate hike decision + forward guidance (12:15 GMT, ~2h from article timestamp)
US PPI data (expected Thursday/Friday)
FOMC meeting (June 17–18)
US-Iran ceasefire negotiations (ongoing, high volatility risk if collapse)
Potential oil/energy supply shock if Strait of Hormuz actually closes
Trump's next trade/tariff announcement (geopolitical wildcard)
screening.risk_warning: If ECB guidance signals 'hold' or easing beyond summer, real yields could fall sharply—bullish for gold; conversely, if Fed hawks signal no cuts through 2026, real yields spike, bearish for gold. Oil shock (major supply disruption) would override real-yield headwind. USD weakness on risk-off reversal could also support gold despite higher rates. Conversely, risk-on rally (equities rally, DXY spikes on safe-haven USD bid) would break gold lower.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in progress and choppy; wait for ECB decision clarity and a retest of $4,090 support or breakout above $4,130 resistance before committing. Do not chase the current modest recovery—late-stage exhaustion risk in a pre-FOMC environment suggests sideways chop into next week's event.

factors.name: Real Yields / TIPS
factors.summary: ECB hiked 25bp and signalled further tightening (Lagarde: 'readiness to tighten further'). Higher-for-longer rate outlook pushes real yields higher, directly bearish for gold. US 10y nominal yields likely to follow if Fed eventually matches or mirrors ECB messaging.

factors.name: US PPI Inflation Print
factors.summary: US PPI jumped to 6.5% y/y (highest since Nov 2022) vs 6.4% expected; m/m surged 1.1% vs 0.7% expected (per FXStreet 12:41 UTC). Hot surprise typically BEARISH gold as it raises odds of sustained Fed hawkishness and higher real yields.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: ECB hiking while Fed remains on hold supports USD strength. Geopolitical uncertainty (Iran-US tensions) and safe-haven flows favour the dollar over gold as relative store of value. EUR/USD under pressure near two-month lows (FXStreet 14:04).

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran-US)
factors.summary: Trump threatens renewed Iran strikes and Kharg Island seizure; Iran retaliating. While historically risk-off (bullish gold), current environment is driving USD strength and equity volatility with selective safe-haven flows favouring dollar and Treasury bonds over gold.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: Oil spikes on Iran tensions, but BNY notes weaker Chinese demand and supply fears are tempering extremes. High energy prices feed inflation expectations, which paradoxically support hawkish Fed/ECB policy that is bearish for gold via higher real yields.

factors.name: Central Bank Policy Divergence
factors.summary: ECB hiking rates for first time since 2023 while Fed data (hotter PPI, jobless claims 229K vs 219K est.) shows labour market still firm. Hawkish ECB messaging and prospect of further tightening support higher rates globally, compressing gold's appeal vs fixed income.

factors.name: Gold Technical / YTD Lows
factors.summary: FXStreet reports gold 'slides to a six-month low on Thursday, wiping out all the gains recorded this year' (12:33 UTC). Momentum is down; technical selling likely ongoing. Price action already extended lower.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations (Lagarde Signal)
factors.summary: Lagarde warned 'short-term inflation expectations have risen' and 'conflict is weighing on activity.' ECB hiking into supply shock signals willingness to sacrifice growth to combat inflation, raising real yields and tightening financial conditions.

factors.name: World Bank Growth Cut
factors.summary: World Bank cuts 2026 global GDP to 2.5% from 2.6%; Eurozone to 0.8% vs 0.9%. Slower growth is mildly bullish for gold, but hawkish central bank response dominates; lower growth + higher rates is bearish for both equities and gold.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment / Equity Markets
factors.summary: US equities drifted higher Thursday despite Iran tensions; chips strong. Risk-off flows are muted; capital rotating into safe havens (USD, Treasuries) rather than gold. SpaceX IPO and AI momentum keep risk appetite alive.

screening.narrative: Gold has been hammered lower, wiping out all 2026 YTD gains and hitting six-month lows. The catalyst: ECB rate hike and hawkish forward guidance combined with a hot US PPI print (6.5% y/y, 1.1% m/m), both signalling higher real yields ahead. The USD is strengthening amid geopolitical uncertainty and central bank tightening divergence (ECB hiking, Fed on hold but data firm). Although Iran-US military escalation typically attracts safe-haven flows, the market is currently prioritising rate expectations and USD strength over gold's traditional hedge role. Technical momentum is decisively bearish; the move down has already extended over several hours.
catalysts_ahead: US Core CPI (next release, likely early June week) — if hot, accelerates gold selloff
Fed speakers (Powell, Waller, Williams) — any hawkish repricing would extend gold weakness
PCE data (early June) — critical for real yield trajectory
Iran-US escalation updates / ceasefire developments — could trigger risk-off rally in gold if sentiment flips sharply
ECB communications (Lagarde further remarks) — any additional hawkishness pins gold down
screening.risk_warning: If Iran-US military conflict escalates into regional crisis (major shipping disruption, oil spike >$150, global supply shock perception), or if Fed pivots dovish unexpectedly or employment data weakens sharply, gold could reverse and reclaim $4,150+. Also monitor real yields on 10y TIPS—if they fall back below 1.5%, that would directly undermine the bearish case.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already extended lower in PHASE_2 on intraday basis; avoid chasing shorts into thin air. Wait for retest of $4,120-$4,130 resistance or a pullback to $4,085-$4,095 support (on any small relief bounce) before entering fresh short. If real yields spike further (TIPS >2.0%), 4,050 becomes tactical downside target.

factors.name: US Dollar strength (Iran deal de-escalation)
factors.summary: Trump cancelled Iran strikes after announcing a peace deal (confirmed by Israel's N12). Risk-off to risk-on pivot triggered broad USD strength, equities rally (+1.3% S&P, +2.4% Russell 2000), and immediate gold weakness. This is a classic safe-haven unwind favoring the dollar.

factors.name: Oil price reversal (WTI down 0.86% to ~$89.50)
factors.summary: WTI retreated sharply as diplomacy tempered Middle East supply fears. Lower energy prices reduce inflation expectations and remove one of gold's structural supports (inflation hedge), pressuring real yields higher and gold lower.

factors.name: PPI inflation surprise (May +1.1% vs +0.7% expected)
factors.summary: Hot PPI print (per Dow Jones) and mention that 'US breakevens should be higher' (Dhingra, BNP Paribas) initially bullish for gold; however, Iran deal news immediately overwhelmed this, suggesting market is pricing in disinflation ahead as energy stabilizes. Near-term, the deal narrative dominates.

factors.name: Real yields trajectory (10y Treasury)
factors.summary: While not explicitly stated, the Iran deal reduces stagflation fears and should support nominal yields while inflation expectations ease, pushing real yields higher. Higher real yields are bearish gold's fundamental driver.

factors.name: Gold technical breakdown / 6-month low
factors.summary: News headline states 'Gold slumps to 6-month low even as inflation fears rise,' indicating technical support collapse and potential momentum selling. Gold trading near lowest level of the year; faltering technical signals cited as headwind.

factors.name: ECB rate hike + potential July pause signal
factors.summary: ECB delivered quarter-point hike on Thursday; sources suggest July pause if energy prices stabilize (now likely post-deal). Neutral for gold: tighter EUR policy is mildly bearish gold, but pause signal suggests peak rate cycle, which is neutral-to-bullish long-term.

factors.name: BoJ rate hike expectations (June likely per ING)
factors.summary: BoJ expected to hike 25bp in June, supporting JPY and potentially strengthening USD/JPY carry unwind pressure on risk assets. Marginally supportive of gold's safe-haven bid, but dominated by dollar strength from risk-on pivot.

factors.name: Risk-on sentiment (equities surge, VIX likely compressed)
factors.summary: Iran deal sparked immediate equity rally (Nasdaq +1.8%), rotations into risk, and likely VIX compression. Gold typically underperforms in strong risk-on regimes absent deflationary shock; this is textbook risk-on environment.

factors.name: Central bank gold buying (structural bid)
factors.summary: Al Jazeera notes gold now accounts for 27% of global reserve holdings, overtaking US Treasuries. However, this is a multi-month/quarterly trend and does not offset the acute intraday/short-term selling from the deal announcement.

factors.name: GLD/IAU ETF flows (not mentioned)
factors.summary: No specific ETF flow data in news window; cannot assess institutional positioning change intraday.

factors.name: Fed policy / FOMC (no recent action, no imminent FOMC)
factors.summary: No FOMC date flagged in next 72h; Fed remains on hold. Powell dot plot and balance sheet status unchanged from prior reads. Not an immediate driver today.

factors.name: Geopolitical tension reversal (primary driver)
factors.summary: The single largest catalyst: US-Iran war fears that were driving gold higher have been defused by Trump's peace deal announcement. This removes the premium gold buyers were paying for tail-risk insurance, triggering sharp liquidation.

key_levels.resistance: $4,200–$4,220 (intraday high before Iran deal news; 50-day MA zone)

screening.narrative: Gold rallied +1.56% in the 4-hour and +1.72% in the 24-hour window on Middle East war fears and hot PPI inflation print; however, the dominant catalyst in the last 2 hours was Trump's announcement of a peace deal with Iran, cancelling scheduled strikes. This triggered an immediate risk-on pivot—equities surged, oil sold off, and the USD strengthened sharply. Gold's safe-haven premium evaporated as markets repriced disinflation and lower tail-risk. A 6-month low was breached intraday, and technical selling has compounded the move. Real yields are likely moving higher as inflation expectations reset lower with energy prices falling.
catalysts_ahead: Fed speakers / Powell remarks on inflation (could shift rate-cut expectations)
Next CPI/PCE release (date not flagged in news, check FOMC calendar)
NFP report (labor market resilience could keep Fed hawkish, bearish gold)
Brent/WTI price action if Iran deal negotiations stall or fresh geopolitical news emerges
ECB July meeting or Lagarde remarks (rate pause signal could ease EUR weakness, weaken USD)
SpaceX IPO settlement (large equity event, marginal risk-on sentiment factor)
US Treasury auctions (30yr bond tailed today; if demand continues soft, nominal yields could rise, bearish gold)
screening.risk_warning: If Iran deal collapses or new geopolitical escalation erupts (North Korea, Taiwan, Russia), gold will reverse sharply higher and this SELL thesis invalidates. Also, if real yields roll over again (e.g., Fed signals a faster/larger rate-cut cycle), or if PPI reacelerates and forces rate-hike expectations, gold could reclaim $4,200+ quickly. Watch for any hawkish Fed speak or hot inflation data to flip the bias back to BULLISH.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE_2 (sharp down move has already printed +1.56% 4h rally wiped out and more, likely -1% to -1.5% from the high). Do not chase shorts aggressively; wait for a retest of $4,180–$4,200 resistance or a failed bounce off $4,140 support to confirm the reversal. Risk/reward is poor for new shorts here; better entry on a relief bounce or if real yields break above recent highs (>+1.8% 10y TIPS).

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY collapsed sharply after Trump cancelled Iran strikes and announced deal near completion. Bloomberg reports 'Dollar Drops the Most in a Month' and FXStreet confirms DXY 'trims earlier gains' to 99.60 after touching three-month high. Weaker USD directly supports gold.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No specific TIPS data in news window. 30-year bond auction showed 5.020% yield (vs 5.008% WI, 1.2 bps tail — weak demand signal per FXStreet). Nominal yields likely easing on geopolitical de-escalation, which should lower real yields if inflation expectations stable.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (US-Iran)
factors.summary: Trump announced Iran deal 'subject to finalization' with signing expected 'next few days'. Market reversed from morning threat of 'VERY HARD' strikes and Kharg Island takeover. Oil plunged to 8-week low (Bloomberg), removing geopolitical premium. De-escalation removes safe-haven demand but reduces inflation expectations.

factors.name: Oil Prices & Inflation Expectations
factors.summary: Global oil settled near 8-week lows after Trump deal announcement (MarketWatch, 2026-06-11 19:28). Lower oil prices reduce near-term inflation expectations, which pressures gold via lower real yield expectations and reduced safe-haven bid.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment & Equities
factors.summary: S&P 500 up 1.86%, Nasdaq +1.8%, Russell 2000 +2.4% post-Iran deal (Investinglive 17:51). Strong equity rally on de-escalation removes gold's safe-haven appeal and encourages rotation from commodities into risk assets. SpaceX $75B IPO also draws liquidity away.

factors.name: Recent Price Action
factors.summary: Gold hit 6-month low of ~$4,023 earlier (FXStreet 17:30), then rebounded to $4,233.80 (+1.45% in 4h, +3.17% in 24h). Bounce is significant but from deeply oversold technicals; move is already in progress.

factors.name: Fed Policy & Rate Expectations
factors.summary: No new FOMC decision or Powell speak in this window. Bond auction weakness (1.2 bps tail) hints market pricing more durational risk. De-escalation may push Fed pause narrative longer, modestly dovish for gold.

factors.name: Inflation Data
factors.summary: Core PPI +0.4% MoM, 4.9% YoY on 2026-06-11 (in-line, per FXStreet). No surprise. Inflation narrative remains subdued; latest prints not supporting higher gold via inflation hedge demand.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows & Central Bank Buying
factors.summary: No GLD/IAU flow data or PBoC/RBI buying announcements in news window. Structural CB bid presumed present but not explicitly triggered.

factors.name: Technical Breakdown
factors.summary: FXStreet (19:06) reports 'Gold slumps to 6-month low even as inflation fears rise…potential interest rate increases and faltering technical signals weigh on prices.' Break below key supports suggests weak technicals despite macro tailwinds.

screening.narrative: Gold initially plunged to a 6-month low of ~$4,023 as Trump's morning Iran strike threat rattled markets and boosted USD/safe-haven demand. The intraday reversal—Trump cancelled strikes and announced deal near completion—triggered a sharp USD collapse (DXY down most in month), oil down to 8-week lows, and a risk-on rally (+1.8% Nasdaq). Gold bounced +3.17% in 24h to $4,233 on weaker USD and de-escalation, but the technicals remain broken (long-term support violated). The move is already underway (Phase 2), and while macro drivers (lower oil, weaker USD, lower real yield expectations) support further upside, the extended rebound risks profit-taking, especially with equities rallying hard and geopolitical premium evaporating.
catalysts_ahead: Trump-Iran deal formal signing (expected 'next few days' per Trump statement, 2026-06-11 20:49)
Potential shift in Fed rate-pause narrative if Iran deal holds (weeks ahead, indirect)
US economic data: any NFP, CPI, or PCE surprise over next 72h
SpaceX IPO trading Friday 2026-06-12 (equity liquidity event, risk sentiment driver)
ECB policy commentary continues post-rate hike (EUR strength headwind for gold)
screening.risk_warning: If Iran deal collapses or fails to be signed as promised, geopolitical risk premium re-inflates, oil spikes, and gold rallies further BUT more likely scenario is deal holds, oil stays low, real yields remain anchored low, and equities continue outperforming—pulling gold lower as safe-haven demand evaporates and rate-cut expectations dim. Technical break below $4,100 could accelerate selling to $3,950–$4,000. Conversely, if USD weakness accelerates further or inflation surprises higher, gold could hold $4,200+ and retest $4,350–$4,400.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended into Phase 2 rebound (up +3% in 24h); avoid chasing rallies here. Wait for pullback to $4,100–$4,150 support before considering fresh long entries; otherwise, shorts into $4,280–$4,300 resistance on continued equities strength and technical weakness.
